nits.issue: POST /v1/approvals/:id is not wrapped in a transaction — UPDATE, INSERT cloud_activity, INSERT approval_rules, and pg_notify are four separate auto-commit round-trips against the tx-mode pooler. A crash between them could leave the approval flipped but no activity/notify (worker would still be reached via 30s status re-query if implemented elsewhere, otherwise it just sits). Acceptable per the comment, but worth a future tightening.

Single POST UPDATE has no `WHERE status='pending'` guard, unlike the bulk path on line 344. If pg_cron flips the row to 'expired' between loadApproval and the UPDATE, this will overwrite 'expired' to the decided status. The earlier loadApproval+expiry checks make this window very narrow (~milliseconds), and the trigger only fires on pending→expired so an extra row state shift to approved/denied still emits the granted/denied activity correctly — but adding `AND status='pending'` then checking rowCount would be safer.
Dynamic `await import('../lib/jwt.js')` on every authorize call is unnecessary — a static import at the top of the file would be cleaner and avoid the per-request resolution cost.

If `Authorization` header is present but malformed (e.g. opaque Supabase JWT), the catch swallows the error and falls through to token check; without `?token=` the response is 401 'unauthorized', which is less informative than 'invalid_token'. Not load-bearing.

`JSON.stringify(row.args_preview)::jsonb` is the right shape for drizzle's postgres-js driver in this repo (cloud-runs.ts uses the same pattern), but worth noting the worker side uses `sql.json()` because it calls postgres-js directly without drizzle. Patterns differ by entry point, both correct.
Test app uses both `app.use('/v1/runs', requireWorkspaceJwt)` AND `app.use('/v1/runs/*', requireWorkspaceJwt)` — mirrors app.ts but the first is dead weight. Style only.

Tests assert SQL query content via `JSON.stringify(sqlObj)` of the drizzle SQL template object. This works today but is brittle to drizzle internals — a live DB integration would be a stronger guarantee. The `verify` block explicitly calls for a live curl session which is still pending (task #9).

approvalChannel logic is duplicated between api and worker (worker/src/approvals/await.ts:78-81). Both must stay in lockstep on the hyphen→underscore rule; a shared helper would reduce drift risk. Not blocking — both implementations are identical and well-commented.
